description: Michael Peterson, struggling with desperation and rage, confronts his former factory boss for work and recognition. The encounter escalates—Mike uses his powers to critically injure the foreman, marking a crucial turning point in his arc as he loses control, witnessed only via news later by the S.H.I.E.L.D. team.

transcript.line: My hands are tied, Mike. It's company policy. You didn't meet quota. I had to let you go.
Gary, look, I got no choice. It's just me and my boy now.
There's no openings, Mike, and even if there were, I got a hundred guys standing in line that are just like you, only they won't bitch when their back hurts.
I was injured, but I'm stronger now. I can handle twice the load I used to.
What are you not hearing? You don't cut it. When I say I need things moved, I need...
Are you out of your mind?!
These are people, Gary. Men! Not parts you replace when they break.
It's not me, man. It's the company. I'm not the bad guy. I'm not the bad guy, man.
